title: Command Preset Delete Confirmation MessageBox Implementation Plan

tasks.title: WindowDialog props and modal stack helper
interfaces: WindowDialogProps optional fields: role, ariaDescribedBy, showCloseButton, resizable, persistGeometry, surfaceClassName
useDialogStack(dialogId: string, active: boolean): { layerIndex: number; isTopmost: boolean }
implementation_steps: Add DialogRole and WindowDialogProps optional fields without breaking existing callers.
Create dialogStack.ts with token-based stack registration and deterministic layerIndex/isTopmost helpers.
Split WindowDialog modal inert lifecycle from topmost focus-trap lifecycle.
Keep inert lifecycle mounted for every modal until that modal unmounts; do not clean it up when isTopmost changes.
Register keydown/focusin handlers only for the topmost modal.
Use exact z-index rules layerZ=5000+layerIndex*20, backdropZ=layerZ, dialogZ=layerZ+1.
Implement showCloseButton=false, resizable=false, persistGeometry=false, role, ariaDescribedBy, and surfaceClassName props.
Create windowDialogModel.ts and windowDialogContract.test.ts for Dialog component contract unit coverage.
Keep existing dialogGeometry.test.ts as geometry utility unit coverage.
Add dialogStack.test.ts and windowDialogContract.test.ts to both test:unit:command-management and lint:command-management scripts.
pitfalls: Do not let lower modal inert cleanup run when MessageBox opens or closes.
Do not allow Escape to close modal or MessageBox.
Topmost backdrop must block clicks to lower Dialog controls.
test_guidance: dialogStack.test.ts success: second modal is topmost.
dialogStack.test.ts failure: removing a middle entry preserves topmost calculation.
dialogStack.test.ts edge: inactive modeless entries do not affect modal stack.
windowDialogContract.test.ts success: default dialog role and enabled close/resize/persistence.
windowDialogContract.test.ts failure: disabled close/resize/persistence props are reflected.
windowDialogContract.test.ts edge: layerIndex=2 produces layerZ=5040, backdropZ=5040, dialogZ=5041.
dod: Existing WindowDialog callers typecheck without changes.
Only topmost modal owns focus trap handlers.
Dialog component unit, geometry utility unit, and dialog stack unit tests run through test:unit:command-management.
test:unit:command-management and lint:command-management include dialogStack.test.ts and windowDialogContract.test.ts.

rollback.command: 1. In types.ts remove DialogRole, WindowDialogBehaviorModel, and WindowDialogProps optional fields added by TASK-P1-001. 2. In WindowDialog.tsx remove dialogStack/windowDialogModel usage, topmost gating, and new optional prop handling. 3. In WindowDialog.css remove z-index and pointer-event changes added by TASK-P1-001. 4. Delete dialogStack.ts, windowDialogModel.ts, dialogStack.test.ts, and windowDialogContract.test.ts. 5. Remove dialogStack.test.ts and windowDialogContract.test.ts from frontend/package.json scripts, then run npm --prefix frontend run typecheck.

tasks.title: MessageBox component and exports
interfaces: MessageBoxProps with dialogId, title, message, okLabel, cancelLabel, okVariant, busy, error, onOk, onCancel
implementation_steps: Add MessageBox-related types to types.ts.
Create messageBoxModel.ts with OK/Cancel/primary defaults and busy normalization.
Create MessageBox.tsx as WindowDialog wrapper using role alertdialog, showCloseButton=false, resizable=false, persistGeometry=false.
Wire ariaDescribedBy to the message element id.
Render Cancel before OK and autofocus Cancel.
Render error with role=alert.
Export MessageBox, model helper, and related types from index.ts.
Add messageBoxContract.test.ts to test:unit:command-management and lint:command-management scripts.
pitfalls: MessageBox must not own feature API calls.
Do not add title bar X or Escape close behavior.
Busy state disables both buttons without changing accessible labels.
test_guidance: messageBoxContract.test.ts success: default labels are OK and Cancel.
messageBoxContract.test.ts failure: busy true maps to disabled-state model.
messageBoxContract.test.ts edge: danger variant and custom labels are preserved.
messageBoxContract.test.ts component contract: role=alertdialog, showCloseButton=false, resizable=false, persistGeometry=false.
dod: MessageBox uses alertdialog and aria-described message.
MessageBox component unit test covers alertdialog/no-X/non-resizable/no-persistence contract.

rollback.command: 1. Delete MessageBox.tsx, MessageBox.css, messageBoxModel.ts, and messageBoxContract.test.ts. 2. Remove MessageBox exports, model helper exports, and MessageBox-related types from index.ts and types.ts. 3. Remove messageBoxContract.test.ts from frontend/package.json scripts, then run npm --prefix frontend run typecheck.

tasks.title: CommandPresetDialog confirmation state
implementation_steps: Import MessageBox from ../dialog.
Replace immediate delete handler with deleteTarget assignment.
Cancel clears deleteTarget/deleteError only when deleteBusy is false.
OK calls deletePreset(deleteTarget.id), clears editing draft if needed, closes MessageBox, and shows 삭제되었습니다 toast.
On delete failure keep MessageBox open and show deleteError in MessageBox alert.
Render MessageBox after WindowDialog with title 삭제 확인, Cancel, OK, and okVariant danger.
Preserve prompt no-execute behavior.
pitfalls: Cancel must not call any server mutation.
Do not move delete error into localError; it belongs inside MessageBox.
MessageBox overlay must block lower CommandPresetDialog controls.
test_guidance: E2E command Cancel leaves item visible.
E2E command OK deletes item and shows toast.
E2E delete failure keeps MessageBox open and item visible.
dod: All preset kinds require MessageBox before delete.
DELETE failure displays MessageBox alert and keeps modal open.

rollback.command: 1. In CommandPresetDialog.tsx remove deleteTarget/deleteBusy/deleteError state and MessageBox render block. 2. Restore handleDelete to immediate deletePreset call. 3. Remove MessageBox-specific CSS from CommandPresetDialog.css. 4. Run npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e:command-management.

tasks.title: Command management E2E delete confirmation coverage
implementation_steps: Use getByRole('alertdialog', { name: '삭제 확인' }) for MessageBox.
Verify aria-describedby points to a message element containing the target label.
Test Escape, backdrop click, and lower .window-dialog-close do not close MessageBox.
Test Cancel branch leaves command item visible.
Test OK branch deletes command, directory, and prompt items.
Intercept one DELETE request with 500 to verify error alert and open-state retention, then unroute.
pitfalls: Unroute the failing DELETE interceptor before cleanup.
Keep e2e-dialog- label prefix to avoid user preset collisions.
Do not rely on CSS class for OK button; accessible name must be OK.
test_guidance: failure: Cancel keeps command and DELETE 500 shows alert.
edge: Escape/backdrop/lower close do not close topmost MessageBox.
dod: E2E validates title, message, Cancel, OK.
E2E validates delete failure alert and open state.
E2E validates MessageBox blocks lower Dialog controls.

rollback.command: 1. Remove MessageBox-specific assertions and DELETE failure route interception from command-management-dialog.spec.ts. 2. Restore the previous prompt delete assertion shape. 3. Remove helper changes from helpers.ts, then run npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e:command-management.
